TextENGNew: ...Tomoe's inquiries at Gogura General Hospital went nowhere, just as she had feared.
No matter how thoroughly she explained the circumstances of the case, the hospital staff stonewalled her, citing the protection of patient privacy.
Without a search warrant, there was simply no way to compel them to disclose the information.
Deflated, Tomoe returned to Kakiuchi the following day.
changeReason: Old adds 'empty-handed' not in Japanese; new 'Deflated' better captures 失意のまま.

TextENGNew: "...I'm sorry.@k@vS11/28/420100968. For dragging you all the way out here."
"No, it's fine.@k@vS11/35/420500132. ...With one train transfer, it only took about half an hour."
Nagisa said this as she took the seat across from Tomoe, who had been waiting inside the restaurant.
changeReason: Old says 'near the front of the store'; new correctly translates 店内 as 'inside the restaurant.'

TextENGNew: ...As it happened, the girl's new address was on the outskirts of Kakiuchi City.
(Natsumi-san, and now Nagisa-san too?@k@vS11/28/420100970. ...It's strange how everything seems to converge on Kakiuchi.)
Before long, a waitress came by and placed a glass of ice water and a menu in front of Nagisa.
"Order whatever you like — my treat.@k@vS11/28/420100972. ...Oh, and I'll have another coffee, please."
"...Could I get a lemon squash?"
After Nagisa timidly asked that, Tomoe nodded to her and told the waitress her order. Then, once the waitress politely bowed and headed back to the kitchen, She turned back to Nagisa once again.

``...I'll cut straight to the point.@k@vS11/28/420100974. Regarding Sawamura-kun's case, it's being transferred from the local precinct to the First Investigation Division at the prefectural police headquarters.@k@vS11/28/420100975. The section chief there is my former boss, and they've agreed to reopen the investigation from scratch as a murder case.``
changeReason: Both versions have minor deviations from the Japanese; old adds 'About what I told you earlier' and new omits 'my former boss' detail.

TextENGNew: "...I see."
Nagisa cradled the glass of ice water in both hands, her gaze drifting down to the shifting surface.
Her eyes remained downcast, heavy with lingering sorrow... but her expression softened slightly, and she breathed a small sigh that might have been relief.

"No matter what I said, they wouldn't listen — kept insisting it was an accident or a suicide.@k@vS11/35/420500136. ...But now they're finally going to investigate it properly.@k@vS11/35/420500137. That alone means so much."
changeReason: Old 'it's different when they hear a detective say it' closely matches Japanese; new changes meaning to 'now they're finally going to investigate it properly.'

TextENGNew: ``......@k@vS11/28/420100977.I'm sorry this is the most I could do.@k@vS11/28/420100978. And also——``
Even if Kouhei's case was reopened as a murder investigation and the culprit eventually identified... he was never coming back.
In the end, all Tomoe had accomplished was unearthing the past...

"...Even so, I'm grateful.@k@vS11/35/420500139. Kouhei-kun was never the kind of person who would take his own life.@k@vS11/35/420500140. So hearing that the case will be reinvestigated... even if nothing changes... it means everything."
changeReason: Old includes 'branded him as someone with mental illness' from Japanese 心の病気にかかっているって決め付けられていました; new omits this. Also, 少しは彼も喜んでくれると思います (a little happy) becomes 'it means everything' in new.

TextENGNew: "...Ozaki-san."
The waitress arrived and set their orders before them.
Nagisa murmured a quiet "Thank you" before inserting her straw and taking a single sip. Then she fell silent, head bowed, absently swirling the ice in her glass.
At a loss for what comfort she could offer, Tomoe wrapped her hands around her cup.
Just then, suddenly.
"Minai-san."
"Eh?"
"I wonder where Reina is right now..."
"Well..."
She suddenly turned away, at a loss for how to respond.
...Perhaps hearing something in the resonance of Tomoe's voice, Nagisa looked up with a start.
"Wait — do you mean...@k@vS11/35/420500144. ...you know where Reina is?"
"You know, don't you?"
"Where is she?@k@vS11/35/420500147. Will you tell me?"
Nagisa leaned forward, almost desperately, pressing the question. Cursing her own carelessness for letting those words slip, Tomoe met the girl's gaze steadily.
"...What will you do if I tell you?"
"I'll go see her."
"Then what?"
"After you meet her... what will you do?"
She had no right to ask and no claim to an answer... but Tomoe couldn't stop herself from pressing on.
"I know how angry you were at Ryuugu-san. I know how deeply you hated her."

``So if you're planning to confront her with that anger... I can't tell you where she is.@k@vS11/28/420100992. I can understand the feeling of wanting revenge when you're consumed by anger and hatred.``
"But when you think about what comes after revenge — do you still feel the same way?@k@vS11/28/420100994. Can you really say it will be worth it?"
changeReason: Old 'more painful... more cruel than you can imagine' translates あなたが想像する以上に苦しくて辛いわよ; new replaces with 'Can you really say it will be worth it?', changing a warning about pain into a question about value.

TextENGNew: "Minai-san..."
...This wasn't somebody else's business.
For eleven years, Tomoe too had carried that same hatred and resentment — kept hidden, burning quietly inside her.
Thanks to that, she fully understood... the pain and emptiness that those emotions entailed.
"So you still hold a grudge against the perpetrator...?"
"It's been 11 years.@k@vS11/30/420200182. I think Yuusuke would be proud of all you've accomplished.@k@vS11/30/420200183. But isn't it about time to move on with your life?"
"...Right now, this is everything to me.@k@vS11/28/420100997. I can't move forward until we solve my parents' murder."
"...@k@vS11/30/420200185.Is that what you're trying to convince yourself of?"
In all honesty... Yamaoki's words had hit Tomoe right where it hurt most.
After all, she was speaking from the bottom of her heart.
"I'll catch the culprit with my own two hands." ...She continued saying that since the day she lost her parents, and it was the single greatest purpose in her life.
But after she arrests the culprit... what then?
What will she be left with?
What will she obtain?
Ever since that conversation with Yamaoki... the words continued echoing inside Tomoe's head.
Maybe he didn't want to share any clues or details related to the case because Tomoe was still motivated by "revenge?"
Were her thoughts on the incident anything other than hatred and resentment...?
(After all this time, I still don't understand.)
From the victim's perspective, what else could she feel other than hatred and enmity?
And besides, wasn't Yamaoki himself the one who encouraged her to become a detective in order to catch the culprit to begin with...?
Tomoe shook her head to bring herself back to reality, then turned to face Nagisa.
"...Ozaki-san.@k@vS11/28/420101000. It wasn't particularly hard to find out Ryuugu-san's location.@k@vS11/28/420101001. Teachers and relatives probably already know."
"Even so, don't you think they avoided telling you because they perhaps wanted to prevent more needless confrontations?"
"It may not be very constructive, but... some things really will settle with time.@k@vS11/28/420101004. I don't think it'll be too late to heal your wounds after you've both calmed down."
"So, if you still feel hatred toward Reina-san...@k@vS11/28/420101006. then you should wait before meeting her."
A look of hesitation floated to Nagisa's face.
She had heard the same thing many times before from adults other than Tomoe... trying to persuade her.
She thought it unlikely that there was any mistake. She also believed that Tomoe cared about her.
And she wasn't sure what the right choice to make was... She was still confused.
However.
"Yes, I probably do.@k@vS11/35/420500155. But... I still want to speak with Reina.@k@vS11/35/420500156. ...I want to talk to her.@k@vS11/35/420500157. Not out of anger... or hate."
Nagisa insistently argued her point.
With that, her eyes had a different color to them than they did the last time Tomoe met her... They were no longer hopeless and despondent.
"Why did she do this?@k@vS11/35/420500159. Why did she leave without saying a thing?@k@vS11/35/420500160. ...There are a mountain of questions I want to ask, and things to discuss with her."
"Maybe the blood will run to our heads just as you say and we'll get into an argument.@k@vS11/35/420500162. I can't deny that possibility."
"But as time goes on, I fear we won't have any more chances to speak to one another."

"Even now, thinking about all of this and imagining things... I realize that it's chasing all the good memories I have of Reina out of my mind and overwriting them with just the bad ones.@k@vS11/35/420500165. Plus, I'm sure she..."
"So, I want to meet and talk with her before all my memories of her turn bad.@k@vS11/35/420500167. ...That's all."
"So please, Minai-san...!"
"...Ozaki-san."
Nagisa turned toward Tomoe and bowed her head all the way down to the table as she said that.
"Minai-san, please tell me.@k@vS11/35/420500170. Where is Reina?"
"Please..."
"...@k@vS11/28/420101011.Can you promise me something?"
After thinking it over for a while, Tomoe realized she couldn't hide it any longer and let out a sigh.
She felt that even if she didn't tell Nagisa anything, she'd be able to find out for herself before long.
In which case, it would probably be best to tell her now while preparing her as best as possible...

"I want you to promise me.@k@vS11/28/420101013. No matter how much hatred and resentment you feel... you'll never resort to the worst thing.@k@vS11/28/420101014. You'll meet and talk with her... and nothing more.@k@vS11/28/420101015. ...Please."
"...Minai-san."
"I know it's unreasonable to ask you to get along as friends.@k@vS11/28/420101017. But... I don't want to see another incident."
"This is a cruel thing to say, but no matter what you do to take revenge, it'll never bring back what you've lost.@k@vS11/28/420101019. So... promise me."
"...Understood. I promise."
After witnessing the calmness in Nagisa's reply, Tomoe strengthened her resolve and tore a page out of her police notebook, then wrote down Reina Ryuugu's address.
Then, she folded it in half and handed it to Nagisa.
"...Hinamizawa village.@k@vS11/35/420500176. So that's where Reina is."
Nagisa checked the contents of the note, then carefully folded it and placed it inside the pouch of her pink purse.
Then, she closed it and quickly dropped her gaze toward a certain item. After being lost in thought for a little while, she sat it down in front of Tomoe and said, "Look at this."
Inside was a small photo tucked into a transparent pocket.
It was a photo probably taken at some sort of excursion, two girls dressed in casual clothes, standing next to each other and smiling.
"What's this...?"
"That's me and Reina.@k@vS11/35/420500178. It was taken at our final elementary school field trip.@k@vS11/35/420500179. After that incident, I burned every photo... except this one."
...After that incident, Nagisa cried so hard it felt like blood would come pouring out of her throat.
Sorrow, anger, hatred, resentment... She wanted to forget everything about Reina Ryuugu.
She wanted to erase her very existence, as though she had never been there at all...
So, she got rid of everything related to Reina.
She gathered all the photos she kept in her photo album and lit them on fire... until they burned to ashes.
And when the sorrow and loneliness resurfaced... she stomped those ashes to bits.
Then, the day before yesterday.
When Tomoe asked her to look around the room for anything that could be considered evidence related to Kouhei's incident... she found a single photo hidden away somewhere.
After picking it up and staring at it for a while... the dark feeling of hatred welled up in Nagisa's chest, once again.
Unforgivable.
Unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable...!!
Then, she attempted to throw it away, she attempted to tear it apart in her hands. But just then.
A single drop fell down on the photo.
When Nagisa recognized it was her own teardrop... she remembered.
Reina Ryuugu was her precious, irreplaceable friend...

"Hey, Nagisa-chan.@k@vS11/02/420700042. We'll always be best friends.@k@vS11/02/420700043. Even after we grow older and move apart... let's have a good relationship, where we can talk about anything."
...That's what Reina told Nagisa at their elementary school graduation ceremony.
When she heard that, Nagisa was happy, delighted... and a little scared.
She didn't want to imagine being separated from Reina.
She wanted to always remain good friends, who could talk to each other any time, every day...
She wanted their current relationship to go on forever, as though time stood still...
"...Uu... uuu... uwaaaaaaaaaah!!"
And so, while holding that photo,.
Nagisa... cried.
Tears came overflowing, and they wouldn't stop...
"...Why did I forget?@k@vS11/35/420500184. Why did I try to forget?@k@vS11/35/420500185. It's far too late to regret it now, though..."
"...You may not believe it, but we were really good friends back then.@k@vS11/35/420500187. We'd go shopping together, and sleep over at each other's houses, talking until late at night..."
There were so many memories.
All of them shined so brightly that she could still remember them clearly, even today...
When she closed her eyes, it seemed like she could hear the laughter in that photo.
"So... I didn't throw this photo away.@k@vS11/35/420500189. We've changed completely, but I didn't want to throw away even the pleasant memories of our time together... That's why..."
"Ozaki-san..."
"Kouhei-kun is there, everyone is there, even Reina is there...@k@vS11/35/420500191. I want to go back to those fun days...@k@vS11/35/420500192. And yet, why...?"
"Uuu, uu... uuu...!!"
Massive tears came spilling out as Nagisa held the purse to her chest.
...Kouhei Sawamura died, and her other classmates apparently transferred to other schools.
And, Reina Ryuugu...
Turning things back to the way they were before was... absolutely impossible.
Frustrated with her helplessness, Tomoe bit her lip and turned her gaze away from Nagisa.
Without her even realizing it... it had begun to rain outside.
A thick, dark cloud covered the sky, and several raindrops were dribbling down the window.
The two of them left the coffee shop and headed toward the train station.
Nagisa had forgotten to bring an umbrella, so Tomoe offered hers to Nagisa and escorted her all the way to the ticket gate to shield her from the rain.
"I took a car here.@k@vS11/28/420101027. You can take that umbrella with you, if you'd like."
"No, I'll be all right.@k@vS11/35/420500195. My mother can pick me up once I arrive at Oumaki Station."
Nagisa shook her head as she said that, and returned Tomoe's folding umbrella to her.

"Thanks for everything, Minai-san.@k@vS11/35/420500197. I'm not fond of the police, but you're completely different, Minai-san.@k@vS11/35/420500198. ...Would it be all right if we talked again some time?"
"Yes, of course."
"I'm glad. ...Well, see you later."
"...Ozaki-san."
After Nagisa bowed her head and turned around, Tomoe called out to stop her. Nagisa tilted her head to the side as Tomoe carefully chose her words and then spoke up with her honest feelings.
"It's a shameful thing to admit, but... I'm still lost, myself."

"Is it the right answer for you and Ryuugu-san to meet again? ...Honestly, I still don't know.@k@vS11/28/420101032. Even though everyone is worried about you and trying to keep you from meeting, I'm doing the opposite."
"So, if you were to ask anyone else they'd probably tell you I'm doing something terribly cold-hearted."

"...But I don't want you treading down the wrong path.@k@vS11/28/420101035. There are a lot of people out there who don't want to see you unhappy... Seriously, a lot.@k@vS11/28/420101036. I realize it's selfish of me to say anything at this point, but even so..."
"Minai-san..."
Many customers passed by the two of them as this scene played out.
They were facing toward one another, staring vacantly for a while... but eventually, it was Nagisa who broke the silence.
"...You're wrong, Minai-san."
"You are a kind person.@k@vS11/35/420500204. No matter what anyone in the world says, that's what I think."
As Nagisa said that... she had a bright smile on her face.

"They often say that it's better to forget the bad things.@k@vS11/35/420500206. So many people have told me that, and I've thought it over so many times.@k@vS11/35/420500207. But that's a mistake... I realized that when I found this photo of Reina."
"A mistake...?"
"Right.@k@vS11/35/420500209. Forgetting something is not the same as hiding it.@k@vS11/35/420500210. ...Even if I turn my eyes away and cover my ears, the scars and memories will still remain."
"So, if you want to forget something, you can't erase it from your mind until you've put it back in order. Otherwise you won't be able to get it out of your heart and mind... That's what I think."
Right... it was only "hidden" away, so the anger and hate never disappeared.
So, in order to stop hiding it... Nagisa thought about Reina once more.
And, she remembered.
Though she did still feel "hatred," simultaneously she felt the desire to "believe" in her...
"I loved Reina.@k@vS11/35/420500213. When I confessed to her that I liked Kouhei-kun, she did everything in her power to support me."
"...No, it wasn't just that.@k@vS11/35/420500215. When I was studying and doing group work, Reina kept busy helping me in all sorts of ways, and stuck with me until the very end."
"That's why... I admired Reina.@k@vS11/35/420500217. I always wanted to be that sort of kind-hearted girl."
Nagisa was proud of Reina Ryuugu.
She wasn't especially proud of herself, and she didn't have much confidence, but she was more proud of the kindness and radiance of her best friend than anyone else.
"But reality wasn't like that.@k@vS11/35/420500219. ...Reina had some major problems at home.@k@vS11/35/420500220. But I never even noticed. She was always doting on me..."

"I was angry and resentful when she betrayed us, and said she wasn't acting like a friend, but I couldn't understand that I wasn't either.@k@vS11/35/420500222. No, thinking back on it now, maybe I was the one unfit to be a friend."
"Ozaki-san..."
"So next time, I want to be a real friend for Reina.@k@vS11/35/420500224. If... she's the Reina I know, she'll regret what she did, and she's probably still suffering from it even now."
"I still hate her and resent what she did...@k@vS11/35/420500226. but in the end, I still want to believe in her."
"And this time, I want to do everything I can.@k@vS11/35/420500228. Talk to each other, argue with each other... and I want to finally forgive her.@k@vS11/35/420500229. That's how I truly feel."
"...You're a kind person."
Tomoe believed that from the bottom of her heart.
Kindness is about having the heart to notice your own mistakes.
And trying to understand and accept the mistakes of others.
So, she prayed and wanted to believe that such a kind-hearted girl would have her wish come true.
"...Thank you so much, Minai-san.@k@vS11/35/420500231. I was only able to realize how I truly felt because you listened to me."
"...Plus, I'm really glad I got to hear what you had to say.@k@vS11/35/420500233. It's thanks to what you said that I was able to remember how much I loved that girl."
"...@k@vS11/28/420101044.But Ryuugu-san was the reason you were even introduced to Sawamura-kun.@k@vS11/28/420101045. And yet... how could such a kind-hearted girl have even considered hurting him?"

"Back then, you told me Reina wasn't that kind of girl.@k@vS11/35/420500235. ...When you said that, I was annoyed that you were defending her... but after I calmed down and thought about it, I realized you were right."
"I'm sure there's something that changed her.@k@vS11/35/420500237. So, I won't one-sidedly blame her. I feel like I want to hear more..."
"...Ozaki-san."
She said it so casually, Tomoe barely even remembered asking that.
However, her pure intentions naturally reached Nagisa, and were able to move her...
Hearing that, Tomoe thought she had actually got through to her.
"Honestly, thanks for everything.@k@vS11/35/420500239. I'll do my best to bring you good news."
"...Yeah.@k@vS11/28/420101048. I pray everything goes well."
"Right.@k@vS11/35/420500241. ...Alright then. Farewell."
Then, the two of them parted ways at the ticket gate.
Tomoe watched her slender frame climb the stairs, then she opened her umbrella and set off to the parking lot in high spirits.
Little by little, the rain slowly transformed into drizzle.
"Well, isn't that a good thing?@k@vS11/29/420400119. Sis, I think you probably did some good for that girl."
"...I sure hope so."
Tomoe sighed after saying that.
A few days had passed since then, but not a single day went by without her thinking of Nagisa.
She apparently attempted to make contact several times, but it was always at times when Tomoe was out.

She considered calling the girl's home at one point, but she thought her parents would get worried if they found out she was having periodic conversations with the police... So in the end, she couldn't do it.
Madoka smiled at her kind-hearted older sister, but still feeling some frustration, slapped her on the back.
"Oww? What was that for?!"
"Don't "what was that for" me!@k@vS11/29/420400121. You've been thinking about this forever. The dice have already been thrown, so wait for fate to handle the rest!"

"...Honestly, all your knowledge is about mundane things.@k@vS11/28/420101052. You should spend some time learning more about the law.@k@vS11/28/420101053. How many times have you received complaints about improperly assigned tickets?"
"The sign was hidden behind a tree. I couldn't help it, ok?@k@vS11/29/420400123. You should direct your criticism at city hall!@k@vS11/29/420400124. Besides..."
"...Huh? Is that you, Minai?"
The two of them immediately halted their friendly argument as soon as they heard a voice calling out to them.
And looking back, Tomoe found Tsukada, who she had recently reunited with back in Tokyo.
"Tsukada-senpai...?"
"Wow, it really is you! Aahahahaha!@k@vS11/33/420800027. What a coincidence... It's a miracle running into you here!"
"Oh, y-yeah..."
During their reunion last week, she did hear that he was going to be in the same prefecture.
However, she never expected to see him again so soon.
"Senpai, are you here for work?"

"Yeah, you could say that.@k@vS11/33/420800029. Chiba-sensei will be giving a lecture in this town next week at the Saeki General Hospital.@k@vS11/33/420800030. I'm on the way back after making arrangements there."
"Is that so...? Then it really does seem miraculous."
But was it more a miracle... or an odd coincidence? It couldn't be.
She considered it for a moment, but found the notion ridiculous and shook her head.
However... Tomoe suddenly noticed something shining in Tsukada's hand, and her mood turned cold.
"...Huh?@k@vS11/33/420800032. By chance, are you Minai's..."
"Her little sister, Madoka.@k@vS11/29/420400127. Long time no see, Tsukada-san."
"Wow, that cute little girl has really grown up.@k@vS11/33/420800034. I remember you always following Minai around with a half-crying look on your face."
"Mind your tongue.@k@vS11/29/420400129. I'm a far more capable person nowadays~."
"...Oh, be quiet."
She spoke harshly... but there was no room to deny what she said.
At any rate, Tsukada and Madoka had met before, back in the old days. Even though she was still an elementary school student at the time... they both remembered each other well.
"By the way, Minai,@k@vS11/33/420800036. do you remember the promise you made last time?"
"Huh?"

"Come on, you said that if we met again we could have tea together, right?@k@vS11/33/420800038. I know this is sudden, but how about going now?@k@vS11/33/420800039. A meal would be fine, too.@k@vS11/33/420800040. It's been a long time, so it would be nice to chat for a little while."
"Oh, umm..."
His somewhat aggressive invitation put her in a defensive posture.
...Although, Tomoe recalled that he was always this way. He was strangely confident and familiar when dealing with women.
Thanks to that, she would probably regret turning down such a long-awaited invitation later on.
(Well, I suppose it's ok.)
Considering the promise she made, She turned to Madoka intending to cancel her shopping plans for today.
"Hey, Mado..."
"Aah, sorry, Tsukada-san.@k@vS11/29/420400131. Sis and I are about to go shopping.@k@vS11/29/420400132. She promised she'd help me pick out some clothes."
"Plus, going on a date after such a sudden meet-up seems like a fling.@k@vS11/29/420400134. Would you mind overlooking her promise, just for today?"
"Madoka...?"
Even though she was speaking politely, Tomoe was confused by how overtly Madoka rejected him.
At the very least, Madoka was very clever at turning the conversation around. In situations like this she'd usually tease her and say, "Well, take your time~" and head home alone.
But... what was the meaning behind it this time?
"Wow, so you've already got a date today with your little sister?@k@vS11/33/420800042. Are you taking the train?"
"No, we took my car today.@k@vS11/29/420400136. It's the red one in the parking lot over there. Can you see it?"
Madoka pointed behind her with her thumb as she said that.
Behind her was a short-term parking lot in front of the station, and Madoka's car was parked there in immediate view.
"I see.@k@vS11/33/420800044. Well, my apologies then."
"I'm just glad I was able to run into you twice after such a long time.@k@vS11/33/420800046. ...Sorry, Minai."
"I-it's ok..."
"Then, let's go out to eat some time when you're free.@k@vS11/33/420800048. Give me a call some time soon.@k@vS11/33/420800049. I gave you my business card before. You've still got it, right?"
"Oh, yes.@k@vS11/28/420101068. I'll contact you again later."
"I'm counting on it.@k@vS11/33/420800051. See you later."
Tsukada quickly left after saying that. After watching him until he made it out of sight, the smile disappeared from Tomoe's face and she stared intently at Madoka beside her.
"...What was that about?"
"Nothing, really."
"Nothing, you say? That man was my senpai in high school and I owe a lot to him, you know?@k@vS11/28/420101071. So, why did you respond like that?"

"I know all that.@k@vS11/29/420400139. He was your senpai in high school, and the captain of the track team.@k@vS11/29/420400140. He was also the student council president, and the self-professed boyfriend of the vice president.@k@vS11/29/420400141. ...Or am I mistaken, Madam Vice President?"
Tomoe remembered it clearly, so she blushed and remained silent.
It had been a long time since she had gotten into a conversation with Madoka about male/female relations. ...Because she was always a little embarrassed about it.
"You... aren't stooping to retaliation, are you?@k@vS11/28/420101074. Because of me getting between you and the chief?"
"That's not it.@k@vS11/29/420400143. ...Well, maybe a little."
With that, Madoka started to think she went too far, so she apologized. Then, she spoke up with a serious expression.

"...I don't like him.@k@vS11/29/420400145. Even from the first time I met him, deep in my gut I could never get a read on what he was thinking.@k@vS11/29/420400146. I couldn't understand why you were going out with him, Sis."
"Madoka...?"
Tomoe was a little surprised to hear Madoka's first impression of Tsukada.
She acted completely indifferent to her sister's love life at the time... but deep down, was that what how she truly felt?
However, Madoka never pressured Tomoe to break up with him.
In fact, compared to herself...
"...I'm sorry."
"Huh? Why are you apologizing?"
"Just speaking without thinking.@k@vS11/28/420101078. But if that was the case, you should've said something when I first started dating him."
"It can't be helped, Sis. You really don't have an eye for men.@k@vS11/29/420400149. If I had said anything negative, it would have only turned into an argument."
"...Ouch, cutting deep."

"There are specific mannerisms that clearly identify the worst kind of men.@k@vS11/29/420400151. Did you notice he had his left hand hidden in his pocket the whole time he was talking to you?@k@vS11/29/420400152. What an obvious trick."

"Don't you think it's odd to suddenly invite an old acquaintance out to eat during an unplanned encounter?@k@vS11/29/420400154. And especially for someone who's married?!@k@vS11/29/420400155. Unthinkable!"
"...Well, I suppose."
From the moment they met, Tomoe did notice that he had a ring on his left ring finger.
It would've been fine if he wore it proudly... but to conceal it while inviting someone out to tea was clearly a shameful act.
"...Are you going to call him?"
"No, I won't.@k@vS11/28/420101082. ...Because there's no reason to go out to eat."
She wasn't lying.
For Tomoe, who had a harsh view on the unfaithfulness of others, she absolutely would have refused a private dinner with a married man.
"Well, maybe she's like Fujita-kun's wife.@k@vS11/28/420101084. A real handful."
"Aa ha ha! I bet."
"He was smiling way too much.@k@vS11/28/420101086. ...Anyway, that's enough talking about senpai.@k@vS11/28/420101087. What would you like for dinner after we're done shopping?"
"Oh, well, there's this one highly recommended restaurant.@k@vS11/29/420400159. I went with Kaoru-san last time, and it was so delicious..."
"...Did you seriously just go there?"
"Ugh? J-just a slip of the tongue..."
She went straight from a conversation about her sister's ex-boyfriend to one about her own lover...
For the time being, Tomoe criticized her remark and headed toward the store with Madoka in a headlock.
"Aah, that was delicious~."
"...Eating five servings is a little excessive.@k@vS11/29/420400162. The couple sitting next to us was staring like you were some kind of alien."
Regrettably, the food at the restaurant they visited was exceptionally delicious.
After all, Yamaoki was an epicurean. The restaurant he recommended the other day, Angel Mort, was a step above the norm.
"I couldn't help it. I was hungry.@k@vS11/28/420101091. Plus, I had that awkward encounter with senpai, so it's perfectly natural for me to be like this."
"You're so stubborn.@k@vS11/29/420400164. Even if you're having regrets, he's a married man, you know?@k@vS11/29/420400165. If you get caught up in an affair, I'll laugh myself to death."
"I know, I was just kidding."
"Sis, your jokes are hard to understand.@k@vS11/29/420400167. ...Haah, I guess you're back to the leftovers-served-over-rice lifestyle."
"Don't be so dramatic.@k@vS11/28/420101094. Oh, would you mind lending me a little money?"
"That's fine, I can borrow it from Kaoru-san.@k@vS11/29/420400169. Sis, shouldn't you be properly saving money on your own? ...Hmm?"
Madoka was sifting through her pockets while talking, but she suddenly stopped and furrowed her eyebrows.
"What's wrong, Madoka?"
"Aah, whoops.@k@vS11/29/420400171. I left my keys in the car..."
"Huuh?@k@vS11/28/420101097. What are you saying? You mean we're locked out?"
"Mmm~, it seems that way.@k@vS11/29/420400173. Tahahaha..."
Apparently, she didn't notice that the key was still in the ignition when she locked the door. ...Madoka made mistakes like that from time to time.
"Seriously... how can you be so careless?@k@vS11/28/420101099. It's common sense to take out the key when you get out of the car."
"Well, the maids at Kaoru-san's house usually take care of that stuff, so I guess that's dulled my senses."
"You liar. He's not that bourgeois.@k@vS11/28/420101101. Didn't you tell me earlier that he only has one housekeeper that started last year?"
"Busted."

Madoka quickly stuck her tongue out and Tomoe lightly poked her on the forehead. Then, she walked to the payphone in front of the parking lot, pulled a 10 yen coin out of her pocket, and tossed it in.
"So, what's the nighttime phone number for the service center?"
"Oh, just a sec.@k@vS11/29/420400177. Uhh, maybe I left it in my car...@k@vS11/29/420400178. Hmm?"
Just then.
When Madoka turned toward the parking lot, she dubiously furrowed her eyebrows and looked closer.
"What's it?"
"Is that... someone by my car?"
She turned to get a good look of her own.
It was dark since there weren't many lights in the area, but she could still make out two... no, three people?
One of them was holding something near the keyhole on the driver's side door, carefully working it.
"Burglars...?!"
"Hey, you guys!!"
Just as she screamed and began to charge at them...
"Wha?!"
"Kyah?!"
A roar rang out as Madoka's car exploded, and the blast sent the two of them flying like dead weight.
Tomoe quickly shielded Madoka as she slammed into the ground and rolled along the asphalt.
Tomoe cautiously raised her head to look around the parking lot... then gasped and froze in shock.
The smell of burning gasoline drifted throughout the entire area.
The hood and windows of Madoka's car... were belching thick plumes of smoke, and flames were emanating from the body of the car.
"Wh-what...?!"
She couldn't understand.
What happened...? She couldn't comprehend it at all.
But what she did understand was that the car was in flames... and if the two of them had gotten into the car, they wouldn't have been safe.
Just then...
Tomoe sensed something behind her and turned back.
On a slightly distant, dimly lit sidewalk in the shadow of a nearby building.
Standing there... was Reina Ryuugu.
"Ah... You? Why?!"
Why are you here?
Why are you staring at me with those cold eyes?
Those questions swirled around inside Tomoe's head... as she stared at the girl's face, illuminated by the flames.
Then.
"...Ahahahahahahahahaha.@k@vS11/02/420700045. Ahahahahahahahahahahaha...!!"
A chill ran through her entire body.
...No doubt about it.
That girl was the one who drained the gas out of Tomoe's car the other day.
And...!
(That girl... did this?!)
With that in mind, Tomoe reflexively began to stand up and apprehend her.
However...
"Madoka...?"
Tomoe suddenly came back to her senses and noticed Madoka waving her arms in a panic.
Her face was pale, and her eyes were open wide as she gazed into the fire.
"S-Sis... a-a fire...!!@k@vS11/29/420400184. N-no... no... noooooooo!!"
"Madoka?@k@vS11/28/420101115. Get a hold of yourself, Madoka!!"
When Tomoe heard footsteps approaching from outside the quiet hospital room, she rose up from the chaise lounge.
That moment, Yamaoki came running around the far corner toward Tomoe, drenched in enough sweat that he probably ran the whole way here.
"...What's the situation?"
"They just administered a sedative and she's currently asleep.@k@vS11/28/420101118. There are no serious injuries, so she'll probably be ok."
"I see..."
Feeling relieved, Yamaoki leaned a hand against the wall and let out a huge sigh, then wiped the sweat from his forehead with his sleeve.
She pulled a handkerchief from her pocket and said, "...Here," while holding it out to him.
"Are you doing ok?"
"I'm fine.@k@vS11/28/420101120. I hit my hips and my elbow when I fell, but they're just bruised."
"...Don't overdo it."
Yamaoki grabbed the handkerchief, then took a seat in the chaise lounge. Tomoe took a seat next to him.
"...Were there any casualties of the incident?"

"Two people are unconscious and in critical condition due to burns sustained across their whole bodies.@k@vS11/30/420200191. One person seems to have been spared, but their mouth and throat were injured, so it's impossible to ask them anything at the moment."
"...@k@vS11/28/420101123.Were any pedestrians or other people nearby injured?"

"Aah, that's one small consolation here.@k@vS11/30/420200193. Several cars were damaged by the blast and shrapnel, but it seems nobody was in the parking lot at the time.@k@vS11/30/420200194. ...It's fortunate that this happened at night.@k@vS11/30/420200195. Things would be far worse if this had happened in the daytime."
Tomoe swallowed her words and kept silent.
It was certainly true that more pedestrians would've been nearby in the daytime, leading to significantly more victims.
However... Madoka probably wouldn't have been as traumatized if hadn't happened at night.
At least then, old wounds wouldn't have resurfaced...
"Chief, do you have any plans after this?"
"No, not particularly.@k@vS11/30/420200197. ...I'd like to stay with Madoka-kun, if possible. Would that be all right?"
...He was probably asking for her permission out of consideration for what happened the other day.
However, Tomoe didn't feel like bringing up such a tiresome subject under the current circumstances.
"...Please do.@k@vS11/28/420101127. I'm going to return to the crime scene.@k@vS11/28/420101128. So, please excuse me."
With that, Tomoe politely bowed then exited the hallway in front of the hospital room.
She felt like she could hear Yamaoki say something... but she kept walking toward the entrance without slowing down.
She didn't want him to see her face right now.
After making her way out of the entrance... she drew a deep breath as the nighttime breeze cooled her head, and then a car roughly put on the brakes to stop right in front of her.
The person who opened the driver's side door and leapt out was none other than Fujita.
"Minai-san, are you all right?@k@vS11/31/421200037. I heard the emergency call from prefectural HQ and came right away..."
"...Perfect timing.@k@vS11/28/420101130. Fujita-kun, can you take me to the crime scene?"
"Huh?@k@vS11/31/421200039. Oh don't worry about that, Minai-san. What about your injuries...?"
"I'm fine.@k@vS11/28/420101132. More importantly, I want to see the crime scene.@k@vS11/28/420101133. ...Because this was probably no accident."
"...You're suggesting it wasn't an accident? Someone intended for the car to explode?!"

"Yes, exactly.@k@vS11/28/420101135. ...I noticed a particularly strong stench of gasoline even before entering the parking lot.@k@vS11/28/420101136. Plus, it's hard to imagine a modern car exploding when the engine isn't even running."
"Even so... what the hell for?@k@vS11/31/421200042. Someone would have to be out of their mind to blow up a car in a parking lot."
"Absolutely.@k@vS11/28/420101138. I don't know if this is an act of terrorism or just some thrill seeker, but I don't even want to understand it."
But... no matter who it was.
Right now, Tomoe was ready to explode with rage against the culprit.
Her precious little sister Madoka.
Tomoe couldn't understand the logic behind it, but she wasn't going to let anyone hurt her...!
And then...
"...Ahahahahahahahahaha.@k@vS11/02/420700047. Ahahahahahahahahahahaha...!!"
...Reina Ryuugu's roar of laughter still lingered in her ears.
She quickly called an ambulance to come pick up Madoka... but when she looked again, the girl was nowhere to be found.
Even though she only dropped her guard for a moment, she still lamented her mistake...
She tightly clenched her jaw.
She still wasn't sure if Reina Ryuugu was involved in this case. In fact, she was dubious whether that actually was Reina Ryuugu herself.
Even so... I swear I'll find this criminal!
Tomoe swore that, deep in her heart.

"...Still, there have been far too many strange car accidents today.@k@vS11/31/421200044. There's another story about a schoolgirl who was struck dead by a truck in Oumaki, but they say she was laying in the middle of the road with her arms and legs spread out."
"What the hell was she thinking?"
"...Oumaki?"
Hearing that, Tomoe couldn't help but ask Fujita to repeat it.
"No, that's all right.@k@vS11/35/420500243. My mother can pick me up once I arrive at Oumaki station."
Oumaki was definitely the place where Nagisa said she lived.
And a female student... died in an accident there?
Just then.

"Contact from Kakiuchi Station transportation division.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_centP2002. We have confirmed the identity of the female student hit by a truck on the highway in Oumaki.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_centP2003. Her name is Nagisa Ozaki.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_centP2004. She suffered cardiopulmonary arrest due to severe cranial compression.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_centP2005. I repeat..."
The voice on the radio was full of static... and it was coming from far away... but she could hear it.
Nagisa... Ozaki...
She was hit by a truck... and died immediately...!!
"...@k@vS11/28/420101142.Fujita-kun, head for Oumaki right away."
"Huh?"
"Hurry!!"
